name: AI safety field-building infrastructure
description: Count of active AI-safety-focused organizations and university groups per country (nonprofit/civil-society orgs and student groups; government institutes and frontier labs excluded). Each entity carries an activity signal and source URL. Counts are a good-faith floor, not a census.
collection_method: web research against aisafety.com directories (communities + map, which flag inactive groups), PauseAI chapter lists, EA Forum, and org websites; assembled 2026-07-22

caveats: US and GB are capped at 15 named entities each (capped=true); true totals are far higher - aisafety.com lists ~50 active US university groups alone and its map lists 321 active orgs globally, the majority US/UK-based. Counts for capped countries reflect named entries only.

Government AI safety institutes (UK AISI, US CAISI, Japan AISI, Korea AISI at ETRI, Canadian AISI, Beijing-AISI, EU AI Office) and frontier labs are excluded - the dataset covers nonprofit/civil-society orgs and student groups. Including government institutes would add 1 entity each to GB, US, JP, KR, CA, CN, IN.

City-level community groups (e.g. AI Safety Melbourne, Toronto AI Safety) are typed 'org' since the schema has no 'community' type; groups anchored at a single university are typed 'university_group'.

Primary evidence base: aisafety.com/communities and aisafety.com/map (actively maintained in 2026 and explicitly flags inactive groups - inactive-flagged entries like Waterloo AIST, Munich MAIA, AI Safety Wuerzburg, LANAIS, and Mexico's AI Futures Fellowship at ITAM were excluded), cross-checked with org websites, EA Forum, PauseAI directory, and country-specific searches.

South Korea: zero non-governmental entities found despite searches for 'AI Safety Korea', 'PauseAI Korea', KAIST/Seoul student alignment groups, and EA Korea AIS programming; Korea's AI safety activity is concentrated in the government AI Safety Institute (ETRI) and academic labs, not field-building orgs.

Saudi Arabia: zero entities found despite searches for AI safety/alignment/existential-risk groups in Riyadh and nationally; activity is governmental (SDAIA participation in the International AI Safety Report) or commercial (Humain), neither of which qualifies.

Russia: entities are informal - Monoid is now online-only after closing its Moscow space, and 'Start in AI Safety' is a Russian-language online community without legal registration; several other Russian-language Telegram/reading communities exist but were not counted as distinct organizations.
China: Concordia AI is the clearest independent field-building org; CnAIDS Network is a state-endorsed academic network counted as one entity rather than its many member university institutes.
Brazil's CEGIA and academic efforts (AKCIT/UFG, CEBRI/ITS-Rio) appear in a June 2024 LatAm mapping but could not be verified as active AIS field-building entities in 2025-2026 and were excluded.

EA national groups were only counted where AI-safety-specific programming was verifiable; some borderline for-profit entities with safety missions (Harmony Intelligence, PRISM Eval, Gradient Institute) are included as orgs.

Counts are a good-faith floor, not a census: groups active only on Discord/WhatsApp with no public activity trail may be undercounted, especially outside English/Spanish/Portuguese-language ecosystems.
